section.heading: Use

subsection.heading: Authorized uses
subsection.chapeau: A participating county, including any applicable agencies of the participating county, shall use county funds, in accordance with this subchapter, only—

paragraph.content: to carry out activities under the Firewise Communities program to provide to homeowners in fire-sensitive ecosystems education on, and assistance with implementing, techniques in home siting, home construction, and home landscaping that can increase the protection of people and property from wildfires;

paragraph.chapeau: to reimburse the participating county for search and rescue and other emergency services, including firefighting and law enforcement patrols, that are—

subparagraph.content: performed on Federal land after the date on which the use was approved under subsection (b); and
paid for by the participating county;

paragraph.content: to cover training costs and equipment purchases directly related to the emergency services described in paragraph (2);
to develop and carry out community wildfire protection plans in coordination with the appropriate Secretary concerned; and

paragraph.chapeau: to provide or expand access to—

subparagraph.content: broadband telecommunications services at local schools; or
the technology and connectivity necessary for students to use a digital learning tool at or outside of a local school campus.

subsection.heading: Proposals
subsection.chapeau: A participating county shall use county funds for a use described in subsection (a) only after a 45-day public comment period, at the beginning of which the participating county shall—

paragraph.content: publish in any publications of local record a proposal that describes the proposed use of the county funds; and
submit the proposal to any resource advisory committee established under section 7125 of this title for the participating county.

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 106–393, title III, § 302, as added Pub. L. 110–343, div. C, title VI, § 601(a), Oct. 3, 2008, 122 Stat. 3909; amended Pub. L. 112–141, div. F, title I, § 100101(a)(9), July 6, 2012, 126 Stat. 906; Pub. L. 115–141, div. O, title IV, § 402, Mar. 23, 2018, 132 Stat. 1079; Pub. L. 117–58, div. D, title XII, § 41202(e), Nov. 15, 2021, 135 Stat. 1134.)

note.heading: Prior Provisions
note.p: A prior section 302 of Pub. L. 106–393 was set out in a note under section 500 of this title prior to repeal by Pub. L. 110–343, div. C, title VI, § 601(a), Oct. 3, 2008, 122 Stat. 3893.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2018—Subsec. (a)(2). Pub. L. 115–141, § 402(1), inserted “and law enforcement patrols” after “including firefighting” in introductory provisions and struck out “and” at end of subpar. (B).
Subsec. (a)(3). Pub. L. 115–141, § 402(4), added par. (3). Former par. (3) redesignated (4).
Pub. L. 115–141, § 402(2), inserted “and carry out” after “develop”.
Subsec. (a)(4). Pub. L. 115–141, § 402(3), redesignated par. (3) as (4).

note.heading: Wage Rate Requirements
note.p: For provisions relating to rates of wages to be paid to laborers and mechanics on projects for construction, alteration, or repair work funded under div. D or an amendment by div. D of Pub. L. 117–58, including authority of Secretary of Labor, see section 18851 of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare.
